title: trachyspermum hispidum
intro: Trachyspermum hispidum, also known as ajwain, is a small shrub-like plant native to the Middle East and India, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The seeds of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their carminative, antispasmodic, and antibacterial properties, making them useful in treating digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and diarrhea. The medicinal properties of Trachyspermum hispidum seeds also contribute to their health benefits, including relieving respiratory problems like coughs and colds, and supporting women's health during menstrual cycles. Trachyspermum hispidum seeds can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or encapsulations, and are often added to teas, soups, or used as a spice in culinary dishes.
